claimText: The GST rate structure has been rationalized into a two-slab system comprising 5% and 18% rates, eliminating the earlier 12% and 28% slabs.
evidenceExcerpt: Two-slab system of 5% and 18%, removing the earlier 12% and 28% rate.
examUse: Statement-based question on the new GST rate structure.
distractorHooks: Stating the new slabs are 12% and 18%
Claiming the 28% slab was retained for luxury goods

claimText: A 40% Special De-Merit Rate is applied to 'sin' goods and select luxuries, which replaces the earlier compensation cess levy.
evidenceExcerpt: A 40% Special De-Merit Rate applies to 'sin' goods and select luxuries, replacing the compensation cess levy.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on taxation of sin goods and the status of compensation cess.
distractorHooks: Claiming compensation cess is levied in addition to the 40% rate
Stating the Special De-Merit Rate is 28%

claimText: The median tax rate under the rationalized GST system has effectively moved from 12% to 5%.
evidenceExcerpt: The median tax rate effectively moves from 12% to 5%.
examUse: Statement-based question on the macroeconomic impact of GST rationalization on the median tax rate.
distractorHooks: Stating the median tax rate increased to 18%
Claiming the median tax rate remained unchanged at 12%

claimText: All individual life and health insurance policies, along with their reinsurance, are exempted from GST.
evidenceExcerpt: GST is exempted on all individual life and health insurance policies and their reinsurance.
examUse: Statement-based question on GST exemptions for the health and insurance sectors.
distractorHooks: Claiming health insurance is taxed at the 5% slab
Stating only life insurance is exempted while health insurance is taxed

claimText: Cement, air conditioners, and all TV sets have been moved from the 28% GST slab to the 18% slab.
evidenceExcerpt: Cement, air conditioners, and all TV sets have dropped from the 28% to the 18% slab.
examUse: Item-slab matching or statement-based question on GST rate reductions for specific consumer goods.
distractorHooks: Claiming cement and ACs are now in the 5% slab
Stating these items are subject to the 40% Special De-Merit Rate
